chunks.text: Ptolemy II Installation Ptolemy II Installation See the Ptolemy II 1.0.1 release notes for details about this release. Ptiny Ptolemy II is available in two versions: Ptiny - which can be used to build models in CT, DE, FSM, PN and SDF using built

models in CT, DE, FSM, PN and SDF using built-in actors. Full - which includes other domains and support for developing custom actors. The Ptiny version is primarily for the Windows platform, it does not include a start up script for Vergil

it does not include a start up script for Vergil for non-Windows platforms. Installation instructions for the Ptiny version can be found online at http://ptolemy.eecs.berkeley.edu/ptolemyII . The Full version is for Windows and Unix platforms. The

ll version is for Windows and Unix platforms. The instructions on this page concern installing the Full version. Instructions for the impatient Install JDK1.3 and have the make command available. Set the PTII environment variable to the top level Pt

the PTII environment variable to the top level Ptolemy directory, that is the directory above this directory bash PTII=c:\\Program\ Files\\Ptolemy\\Ptolemy\ II\ 1.0.1 export PTII csh setenv PTII /users/yourname/ptII Run configure cd "$PTII" rm -f c

ers/yourname/ptII Run configure cd "$PTII" rm -f config.* ./configure Ptolemy is shipped with .class and .jar files, but the $PTII/bin/scripts needs to be updated. The safest thing to do is to run make install on the entire tree: make install Start

ake install on the entire tree: make install Start vergil $PTII/bin/vergil Requirements The PTII variable The PTII environment variable is used to refer to the directory that contains the Ptolemy II tree. Setting PTII is covered in the Unix and Windo

ree. Setting PTII is covered in the Unix and Windows sections below. Java Ptolemy II has been developed under JDK1.3.0, which is available from http://java.sun.com/j2se/1.3/ Ptolemy II has also been successfully built and run with JDK1.2.2. We recomm

uccessfully built and run with JDK1.2.2. We recommend JDK1.3 over JDK1.2.2. If you have problems with under JDK1.2.2, please upgrade to JDK1.3. Java Plug-in The applet tags have been configured to require JDK1.3, so to run the applets in Netscape 4.

quire JDK1.3, so to run the applets in Netscape 4.7 or Internet Explorer 5.0 under Windows, you will need to use the Java Plug-in Currently, the Java Plug-in 1.3 is included in the JDK1.3 download for Windows. If you are running under Solaris 2.6 or

Windows. If you are running under Solaris 2.6 or 2.7, you can download a separate plug-in or a complete Netscape installation. If your platform is not supported by the plug-in, you can run the applets with the JDK appletviewer command. See the Plug-

s with the JDK appletviewer command. See the Plug-in section of the Ptolemy II Troubleshooting guide for details. Optional packages that are shipped with Ptolemy Jacl Jacl is a 100% Java implementation of Tcl that we use for testing Ptolemy II. Each

n of Tcl that we use for testing Ptolemy II. Each Java directory contains a test subdirectory that contains Tcl files that use Jacl to test the Ptolemy II Java code. Jacl is only necessary if you are planning on running the Ptolemy II test suite. We

planning on running the Ptolemy II test suite. We ship a customized version of Jacl called Ptjacl. The primary difference between Ptjacl and Jacl1.1 is that Ptjacl is shipped as one jar file. The Ptjacl jar file at $PTII/lib/ptjacl.jar is shipped wi

acl jar file at $PTII/lib/ptjacl.jar is shipped with Ptolemy II. For more information about Jacl and Tcl Blend, see Scriptics Jacl and Tcl Blend page . Christopher Hylands' Java Page. java:: man page Diva Diva is a visualization package used by some

Diva Diva is a visualization package used by some of the demos. The Ptolemy II distribution include $PTII/lib/diva.jar . The Diva sources and documentation are available at http://www.gigascale.org/diva . Optional packages not shipped with Ptolemy Ja

va . Optional packages not shipped with Ptolemy Java3d The 3-D graphics (GR) domain demos require Java3d, which can be installed from http://java.sun.com/products/java-media/3D/ . You need not install Java3d if you are not planning on using GR domai

l Java3d if you are not planning on using GR domain. JavaCC JavaCC is the Java Compiler Compiler, which similar to yacc and lex . JavaCC is only necessary if you modify the expression parser in ptolemy/data/expr . As of 1/00, Ptolemy II was using Jav

y/data/expr . As of 1/00, Ptolemy II was using JavaCC 1.1 To install JavaCC, follow the instructions below Download JavaCC1_1.class from http://www.sun.com/forte/ffj/resources/contributedcontent.html Set your CLASSPATH to . setenv CLASSPATH . cd to t

Set your CLASSPATH to . setenv CLASSPATH . cd to the directory where the JavaCC .class file was downloaded to. Run java on the file. Note that you should not include the .class suffix in the command line. java Java1_1 $PTII/configure looks for JavaC

line. java Java1_1 $PTII/configure looks for JavaCC in the $PTII/vendors/sun/JavaCC directory. If configure does not find JavaCC there, then it looks for the javacc binary your path. You may also use the --with-javacc=DIR configure argument. The be

e the --with-javacc=DIR configure argument. The best place to install JavaCC is $PTII/vendors/sun/javaCC Rebuilding The Ptolemy II distribution includes the compiled Java .class files and the javadoc output, so it is not necessary to 'build' or run m

output, so it is not necessary to 'build' or run make to use Ptolemy II. Unix If you would like to rebuild from the Java sources under Unix, you should set the PTII environment variable in your environment. Under C Shell: setenv PTII /users/ptII You

nment. Under C Shell: setenv PTII /users/ptII You may want to add that line to your ~/.cshrc file. To configure the makefiles, run $PTII/configure , which is a GNU autoconf script that looks for your Java compiler, reads in $PTII/mk/ptII.mk.in and g

Java compiler, reads in $PTII/mk/ptII.mk.in and generates $PTII/mk/ptII.mk : cd "$PTII" rm -f config.* ./configure $PTII/configure creates $PTII/config.status and $PTII/config.cache . If you would like to reconfigure Ptolemy II to use a different J

ike to reconfigure Ptolemy II to use a different Java compiler, set your path so that the appropriate compiler is first in your path, then remove config.status and rerun configure. You may also use the --with-java= configure command line argument.

he --with-java= configure command line argument. For more information, run configure -help . To build: make Windows Ptolemy II was developed under Windows 2000. We have successfully installed Ptolemy II under Windows '98, but if you have problems u

y II under Windows '98, but if you have problems under Windows '98 we recommend upgrading Windows 2000. To build Ptolemy II under Windows 2000: Install JDK1.3 from http://java.sun.com/j2se/1.3/ Install the Cygwin toolkit from http://sources.redhat.c

ll the Cygwin toolkit from http://sources.redhat.com/cygwin/ Complete installation instructions can be found at http://sources.redhat.com/cygwin/faq , below is a summary: Create an empty directory Download http://sources.redhat.com/cygwin/setup.exe a

nload http://sources.redhat.com/cygwin/setup.exe and save it in the empty directory Click on setup Select Install from Internet Use the default Local Package Directory, which should be the directory you created above. Select c:\cygwin as your instal

you created above. Select c:\cygwin as your install root directory. Select 'Default Text File Type' of DOS. If you are not behind a firewall, click on direct connection. Select a nearby mirror. You will be presented with a list of packages to install

ll be presented with a list of packages to install. A complete installation takes at least 75Mb. At the minimum, select the packages below, which is roughly a 3Mb download that expands into an 8.7Mb installation. ash - For /bin/sh bash cygwin dif

b installation. ash - For /bin/sh bash cygwin diff - Configure uses cmp fileutils - For ls grep - Used by configure make sed - Needed by config.status, which is created by configure shellutils - For basename textutils - For cat and tr If you are ser

basename textutils - For cat and tr If you are serious about development you may also want: autoconf - requires gawk, grep, m4 findutils gzip less tar Rebuilding in java/lang/ptbyacc requires gcc and w32api Set the value of the PTII environment varia

w32api Set the value of the PTII environment variable to the name of the directory where Ptolemy II is installed. Under Windows 2000, you set the environment variable via the Environment tab of the System control panel ( Start Menu -> Settings -> Con

stem control panel ( Start Menu -> Settings -> Control Panels -> System -> Advanced -> Environment Variables The PTII value should be set using back slashes so that javac gets the right path if you've installed Cygwin in a location other than C:\ suc

installed Cygwin in a location other than C:\ such as C:\Cygwin . An example PTII value would be: c:\users\yourname\ptII In the Environment tab, you should also set your path to include the location of the JDK bin directory (i.e. C:\jdk1.3\bin ) Whe

of the JDK bin directory (i.e. C:\jdk1.3\bin ) When you run cygwin.bat, your path should be set to include the Cygwin tools. You can test this by typing type make , which should return make is /usr/bin/make If make cannot be found, then you may also

in/make If make cannot be found, then you may also need to add the Cygwin bin directory c:\cygwin\usr\bin to your path. Optional: Install JavaCC, see the instructions above . Start up Cygwin bash , and cd to the Ptolemy II directory. Run rm -f config

d cd to the Ptolemy II directory. Run rm -f config.* Run ./configure Run make Currently, Ptolemy II does not support building via Microsoft Visual Studio nmake under Windows. Also, we have not tried building Ptolemy II under Windows '95 or Windows '

building Ptolemy II under Windows '95 or Windows '98. The instructions for setting up a parallel tree using CVS can be found in $PTII/doc/coding/develsetup . Last Updated: $Date: 2001/03/19 05:19:00 $
